project.name: Knotz Raven Mayhem
project.productGoal: Move Knotz Raven Mayhem from a playable beta candidate into an official version 1 release by raising the visual, effects, shader, GFX, and presentation quality while preserving the now-working desktop and landscape-mobile gameplay baseline.
project.v1ScopeDecision: Local records and local progression only. Online leaderboards, databases, backend accounts, and cloud saves remain deferred beyond v1.

handoffSummary.nextTeamMission: Own the v1 presentation pass: special effects, GFX, shader-style treatments, stronger stage identity, hit/weapon feedback, boss spectacle, and release-grade visual polish.
handoffSummary.whyThisMatters: The game loop, armory progression, desktop layout, landscape-mobile flow, local saves, release shell, and deployment path are in good shape. The biggest remaining difference between beta and official v1 is production feel: the game needs more visual punch, clearer feedback, and more memorable stage/boss presentation.
handoffSummary.workingBaseline: Treat the current desktop and landscape-mobile experience as accepted baseline behavior unless new bugs appear. Do not restart the mobile workstream; protect it while improving effects and presentation.
handoffSummary.latestKnownGate: Recent local checks passed individually: npm.cmd run typecheck, npm.cmd run build, npm.cmd run release:check, and npm.cmd run release:smoke. The release check signal was Release check passed: linkedAssets=3 seedAssets=4 releaseShellAssets=10. The served dist smoke signal was Dist smoke passed: basePath=/Knotz-Raven-Mayhem/ linkedAssets=3 seedAssets=4 releaseShellAssets=10.
handoffSummary.handoffWarning: CI verification does not publish the live game. The GitHub Pages site updates only after the Deploy GitHub Pages workflow is manually run with workflow_dispatch on master.

currentState.summary: The original raven click-target prototype has been rebuilt into a Phaser 3 + TypeScript + Vite arcade shooter with staged runs, bosses, Jackpot Alley bonus pacing, between-stage interstitials, armory-forward game over, persistent local saves, DOM overlays, idle cabinet demo/tutorial screens, procedural stage art, stage-aware procedural audio, pooled transient effects, procedural scoring/armory/bonus-round FX, SEO/web kit release assets, release verification scripts, GitHub Actions workflow scaffolding, and a landscape-first mobile layout.

implementedContent.stages: 9 named stages with different spawn pools, palettes, speed multipliers, rewards, procedural set dressing, a Jackpot Alley bonus round, overflow scaling after the final named stage, and field-drain stage transitions that wait for active ravens and powerups before showing stage clear.
implementedContent.enemies: 11 enemy definitions: normal, fast, golden, armored, mini, shield, splitter, dive, wraith, brute, and boss. Stage pools reveal enemy types gradually across the full 9-stage arc.
implementedContent.weapons: 4 guns: Quarter Pistol, Burst Rifle, Scattergun, Arc Laser.
implementedContent.assistChips: 4 Assist Chip unlocks: Stock Cabinet, Turbo Capacitor, Threat Scanner, Chaos Net. These tune recharge feel and target forgiveness while the live reticle identity follows the equipped gun.
implementedContent.upgrades: 4 permanent upgrade tracks: cooldown, combo window, starting lives, payout scaling.
implementedContent.powerups: 6 powerups: slow-mo, multi-shot, score boost, extra life, overdrive, coin rush.
implementedContent.audio: Procedural WebAudio with menu/run/boss modes, stage-aware motifs, weapon-specific shots, enemy-specific hit cues, powerup cues, boss warning/defeat stings, stage clear, miss, and game over. AudioContext creation is gated until a real user gesture.
implementedContent.visuals: Seed raven/explosion assets plus procedural stage set dressing, cabinet-style HUD framing, weapon-driven reticles, Assist Chip effect labels, cooldown feedback, projectile traces, multishot chain traces, shotgun spread visualization, laser lane feedback, scoring bursts, coin showers, Jackpot Alley ambient FX, and pooled transient effects for long-session performance.
implementedContent.mobile: Landscape-first mobile is now the accepted mobile direction. Portrait phones show a rotate gate, the manifest requests landscape, HUD density and enemy visual scale were reduced for phone landscape, and Armory uses a phone-landscape page scroll model instead of nested card scrolling.
implementedContent.releaseOps: Release verification scripts, balance report, SEO/release-shell asset checks, served dist smoke for /Knotz-Raven-Mayhem/, GitHub Actions CI, and manual GitHub Pages deployment workflow are in place.
implementedContent.pwaShell: index.html and public/ include mobile web app tags, icons, manifest.webmanifest, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, social-preview.png, and canonical metadata. The manifest uses start_url './', scope './', display 'standalone', orientation 'landscape', and same-origin id './'.

latestPlaytestUpdate.summary: Mobile is now working well enough to hand off. iPad and laptop layouts are good. Phone landscape supports menu/options/play, and Armory has been reworked as a whole-page mobile scroll surface. Remaining work should focus on v1 effects, GFX, shader-style presentation, and final release polish.
acceptedSignals: Desktop and iPad gameplay remain strong.
Options, core menu navigation, and gameplay work in landscape mobile testing.
Game-over-to-armory flow makes sense and should remain a progression anchor.
Between-stage interstitials and stage drain behavior should be preserved.
Recent console issues were addressed: manifest id is same-origin, AudioContext is user-gesture gated, and the explosion animation uses valid frames 0-4.
remainingValidation: Re-test Armory page scrolling on the user's target phones after the latest page-scroll change.
Run final real-device checks after major effects/shader work because visual load can affect phone performance.
Repeat published-build fresh-save and returning-save checks after deployment.
Run DOCS/RELEASE-QA-CHECKLIST.md end to end before calling v1 final.

knownUnfinishedAreas: The game needs a full v1 GFX and special-effects pass to feel release-grade rather than beta-simple.
Stage identities need more memorable foreground/background effects, transitions, and danger/readability cues.
Weapon impacts, combo moments, powerups, boss warnings, boss damage, and boss defeats need stronger payoff.
Shader-style treatments are currently minimal. Any shader/WebGL work should be performance-conscious and degrade cleanly on mobile.
Current seed raven and explosion assets are functional but not final production art.
Armory is planned to grow after beta; its mobile scroll model should be preserved and retested as content expands.
Final mobile performance validation remains necessary after the effects pass.

nextTeamPrimaryFocus.theme: Version 1 effects, GFX, shader-style polish, and release-grade presentation.

priorityOrder.title: Create a v1 visual effects language
details: Define a consistent arcade-neon effects style for hits, misses, coins, combo chains, powerups, stage clears, boss warnings, and game over.
Keep effects readable over the moving playfield; spectacle should not hide ravens, powerups, the reticle, or important HUD information.
Reuse Phaser Graphics, pooled transient objects, existing sprite assets, and lightweight procedural effects before adding heavy assets.
Respect reduced-motion settings and screen-shake settings.
Keep effects data-driven or centralized enough that future content can reuse them instead of duplicating one-off scene code.

priorityOrder.title: Upgrade weapon and enemy feedback
details: Make each weapon feel visually distinct: pistol precision, burst cadence, shotgun spread, and arc laser lane identity should read instantly.
Strengthen enemy hit and destruction feedback by archetype: armor, shield, splitter, wraith, brute, golden, and boss should not all feel like the same hit effect.
Improve combo escalation feedback without cluttering the center or lower-middle playfield.
Use pooled effects and caps; do not create unbounded particles or long-lived graphics objects during late-stage runs.
Do not change weapon economy, upgrade costs, or stage balance unless an effect pass exposes a concrete gameplay readability blocker.

priorityOrder.title: Make stages and bosses feel like v1 set pieces
details: Give each major stage a stronger mood through layered background animation, haze, lighting pulses, foreground silhouettes, warning flashes, or shader-style overlays.
Make Jackpot Alley feel like a special bonus moment with distinct visual rhythm and reward feedback.
Make boss entries, damage states, and defeats feel larger and more intentional.
Keep stage effects behind gameplay readability. The player must still read raven silhouettes and tap targets on phones.
Document any new stage-effect architecture so later stages can be authored without guessing.

priorityOrder.title: Evaluate shader and post-processing options safely
details: Explore Phaser pipeline/shader-style effects only where they materially improve the game: CRT treatment, heat shimmer, lightning, boss aura, laser bloom, or stage haze.
Prefer opt-in, scoped shader effects over global post-processing that risks mobile performance or browser compatibility.
Provide a fallback path for mobile, reduced-motion, or weaker GPUs.
Test shader changes on desktop and phone landscape before merging.
Avoid service-worker/offline scope unless explicitly approved; v1 should not risk stale cached JS chunks.

priorityOrder.title: Protect release confidence
details: Run npm run typecheck and npm run build after code changes.
Run npm run balance:report if gameplay timing, rewards, enemy behavior, or economy changes.
Run npm run release:check and npm run release:smoke after release-shell, asset, Vite, public, manifest, or dist path changes.
Run npm run release:verify before handoff or release candidate tagging.
After deploy, validate the live GitHub Pages URL in a fresh browser profile or private window.

currentBacklogFromDocs: Re-test Armory scrolling on real target phones after the latest mobile page-scroll change.
Create a v1 visual-effects pass for weapon shots, impacts, kills, combos, powerups, boss warnings, boss defeats, stage clears, and game over.
Add stronger stage-specific GFX and shader-style atmosphere while preserving raven/reticle readability.
Improve boss spectacle and Jackpot Alley reward presentation.
Profile long-session phone performance after effects work; watch particle counts, pooled graphics reuse, CPU/GPU pressure, and late-stage readability.
Run DOCS/RELEASE-QA-CHECKLIST.md end to end before final v1 release.
Complete published-build fresh-save and returning-save checks after deployment.

recommendedFirstSprint.slice: V1 effects architecture and weapon-impact pass
recommendedFirstSprint.reason: The next biggest v1 gap is feel and presentation. Weapon and impact feedback are high-frequency moments that will make the whole game feel more official quickly.
acceptance: Each weapon has visibly distinct shot and impact feedback.
Enemy archetypes have readable hit/destroy cues without obscuring targets.
Effects are pooled or capped and do not accumulate during long sessions.
Reduced-motion settings reduce non-essential motion and flashes.
npm run typecheck and npm run build pass.

recommendedFirstSprint.reason: The game already has nine stages and recurring boss fights; v1 needs those moments to feel authored and memorable instead of simple palette swaps.
acceptance: At least three early-to-mid stages have stronger distinctive visual identity.
Boss warning, boss active state, boss damage, and boss defeat each have upgraded presentation.
Jackpot Alley has clearer bonus-round visual treatment.
Phone landscape remains readable and playable.
npm run release:smoke passes after asset/runtime changes.

recommendedFirstSprint.reason: Shader and post-processing effects can raise the official v1 feel, but they must be scoped and validated carefully for mobile.
acceptance: One or two scoped shader-style effects are prototyped behind clear runtime conditions.
Effects have a fallback or can be disabled without breaking gameplay.
No global shader effect makes phone gameplay harder to read.
Reduced-motion and mobile performance are explicitly considered.
npm run typecheck and npm run build pass.

architecturePointers.runtime: src/main.ts creates the Phaser game with BootScene, AttractScene, and GameScene and imports src/styles.css.
architecturePointers.boot: src/game/scenes/BootScene.ts loads seed sprites/audio and creates raven-flap plus boom-pop animations. boom.png currently has five frames, indexed 0-4.
architecturePointers.gameplayScene: src/game/scenes/GameScene.ts owns active run spawning, input, hit resolution, touch/crosshair behavior, stage flow, boss flow, background set dressing, procedural effects, pooled transient effects, and HUD dispatch. This is the primary v1 effects/GFX work file.
architecturePointers.menuScene: src/game/scenes/AttractScene.ts owns attract screen state, idle cabinet demo rotation, armory/records/options/credits commands, save mutations, menu music, pooled title-screen ravens, and armory transaction FX.
architecturePointers.uiLayer: src/ui/app.ts renders DOM overlays for attract, armory, records, options, HUD, stage clear, pause, and game over. src/ui/events.ts is the scene/UI command bridge.
architecturePointers.styles: src/styles.css controls the responsive DOM overlay, HUD placement, modal sizing, phone landscape layout, Armory scroll model, reduced-motion CSS, safe-area padding, and static SEO fallback styling.
architecturePointers.releaseShell: index.html and public/ are the primary places for SEO, Open Graph/Twitter metadata, favicon/app icons, web manifest, social preview images, mobile web app tags, robots, and sitemap.
architecturePointers.pwaManifest: public/manifest.webmanifest currently sets id './', start_url './', scope './', display 'standalone', orientation 'landscape', and icon paths under public/icons/.
architecturePointers.data: src/game/data/stages.ts, enemies.ts, weapons.ts, upgrades.ts, and tuning.ts are the main data/tuning entry points. Avoid changing balance constants for visual-only work.
architecturePointers.audio: src/game/systems/ArcadeAudio.ts owns procedural WebAudio and now waits for a user gesture before creating/resuming AudioContext.
architecturePointers.save: src/game/save.ts normalizes localStorage save data and applies purchases/rewards. Preserve returning saves unless v1 explicitly changes save shape.
architecturePointers.balance: scripts/report-balance.mjs reads live TypeScript data and estimates stage pacing, rewards, and economy costs.
architecturePointers.releaseChecks: scripts/check-release.mjs validates dist files, release-shell metadata, and public web kit assets. scripts/smoke-dist.mjs serves dist from /Knotz-Raven-Mayhem/ and fetches linked assets, seed assets, and release-shell assets.

verificationStatusAtHandoff.lastKnownPassingReleaseCheck: npm.cmd run release:check printed Release check passed: linkedAssets=3 seedAssets=4 releaseShellAssets=10. In one run, the shell wrapper timed out after the passing output; rerun if a clean process exit is required.
verificationStatusAtHandoff.lastKnownReleaseSmokeSignal: Dist smoke passed: basePath=/Knotz-Raven-Mayhem/ linkedAssets=3 seedAssets=4 releaseShellAssets=10
recentManualPlaytestSignals: Laptop and iPad layouts work well.
Landscape mobile can change options and play the game.
Armory is a major progression surface and must remain usable as more content is added.
User wants the next phase to focus on special effects, GFX, shader effects, and official v1 release presentation.
releaseVerifyCoverage: release-shell metadata and public web kit asset verifier
manualValidationStillRequired: Re-test latest Armory scroll behavior on real target phones.
Real iPhone landscape validation after the effects pass.
Real Android landscape validation after the effects pass.
PWA install and standalone launch checks on iOS Safari and Android Chrome if v1 will promote installability.
Fresh-save and returning-save checks from browser and installed app.
Final full release QA checklist after v1 effects work.

recommendedSubagents.name: VFX Systems Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Own reusable hit, kill, combo, coin, powerup, stage-clear, and game-over effect systems in GameScene.ts. Keep effects pooled, capped, reduced-motion aware, and reusable for future content.

recommendedSubagents.name: Weapon And Enemy Feel Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Own distinct visual feedback for weapons and enemy archetypes, including projectile traces, impact bursts, armor/shield/wraith/splitter treatments, and boss damage readability.

recommendedSubagents.name: Stage And Boss GFX Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Own stage atmosphere, background/foreground motion, boss warning/entry/defeat spectacle, Jackpot Alley presentation, and stage-specific visual identity.

recommendedSubagents.name: Shader And Performance Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Evaluate Phaser shader/pipeline or shader-style effects, add fallbacks, profile phone landscape performance, and prevent visual polish from damaging readability or frame rate.

recommendedSubagents.name: Release QA And Deployment Agent
recommendedSubagents.focus: Own npm run release:verify, release:smoke, published-build validation, fresh-save/returning-save checks, GitHub Pages Actions deployment, and final v1 QA evidence.

githubPagesDeploymentNote.currentStatus: The correct publish path remains manual Deploy GitHub Pages on master after release verification is green. CI verification alone does not publish.
githubPagesDeploymentNote.why: The classic Pages branch/folder source can show a blank white screen after the Vite migration because it may serve stale or mismatched static output. The Actions deployment uploads the freshly built dist artifact.

implementationGuidance: The next team's main mission is v1 visual polish: special effects, GFX, shader-style treatments, stage identity, boss spectacle, and release presentation.
Do not restart mobile/PWA as the main workstream. Preserve the accepted landscape-mobile baseline and re-test it after effects changes.
Do not drift into online leaderboards, accounts, databases, or cloud systems for v1.
Preserve the current gameplay loop, armory economy, stage pacing, and local save behavior unless the user explicitly approves tuning changes.
Use DOM overlays for text-heavy UI such as armory, records, options, stage-clear summaries, upgrade recommendations, and game-over prompts.
Use Phaser graphics/sprites/pipelines for playfield effects, shader-style treatments, touch/crosshair feedback, projectile traces, impact bursts, stage/boss presentation, and reward moments.
Use only existing assets under public/assets/ or procedural Phaser/CSS work unless the user explicitly approves new asset production.
If adding real shader/pipeline effects, keep them scoped, test on phone landscape, and provide fallback behavior.
If adding new assets, keep paths compatible with the /Knotz-Raven-Mayhem/ base path and rerun release:check plus release:smoke.
When save shape changes, update normalization in src/game/save.ts and test returning-save behavior manually.
After content or tuning changes, rerun npm run balance:report and update DOCS/BALANCE-NOTES.md if the baseline changes materially.
After runtime, asset, manifest, release-shell, Vite config, or deploy-script changes, run npm run release:verify before handoff.
After deployment, validate the live GitHub Pages URL in a fresh browser profile or private window.
openDesignQuestions: Which three stage identities should receive the first v1 GFX pass?
Should shader-style effects be global atmosphere, weapon-specific, boss-specific, or stage-specific?
How intense should screen shake, bloom-like glow, and flash feedback be on phones versus desktop?
Should future Armory expansion add more weapons/chips before v1, or should v1 polish the existing loadout set first?
Should v1 include new production art assets, or stay procedural/seed-asset based until after launch?
What is the desired final benchmark for phone performance during late-stage runs with effects enabled?
